IBEW.text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is running the hottest board for pure money right now. **$62.51/hr** across four contractors (Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett) all feeding the Microsoft Mount Pleasant data center build. That's top-tier scale in the Midwest. Only 11 calls against 76 hands means Book 1 is nearly empty — if you're in, you're on a job same day. Long sleeves and safety toes non-negotiable; Wisconsin inside JW card or 2+ years verifiable preferred.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has 104 hands chasing just 8 calls on the Lake Mariner Data complex — serious board crush. Ferguson and O'Connell Electric are running **$48.20/hr** with per diem available, 7/10s, and orientation slots Mon/Tue/Thu or Mondays only. First shift, so you're not flipping your sleep schedule. Tight ratio means you move fast.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is paying **$44.80/hr** but sweetening the pot hard: **$100/day non-taxed per diem** (GPS-verified 100+ mile radius), plus **$100 weekday incentive / $150 weekend bonus**, and another $100 if you grab the ND license. Parsons Electric dominates the Polaris Forge Data Center calls — the math works if you're chasing volume and don't mind cold. 31.4% shift differential on nights.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** shows **$10/hr over scale** on three separate Port Washington Data Center REPOST calls (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Uihlein) — staggered 6am and 7:30am starts across Job#041, #136, #090. 38 calls, 182 hands. That repost churn signals turnover, not shortage, but the premium is real.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** has 35 calls and **$48.00/hr top scale**, but Miller Electrical's Cable Splicer role at QTS Fayette hits **$40.90/hr** requiring 3 years documented — hard-ticket work. Inglett & Stubbs running Wellstar and QTS on 4-10s and split 8s across weekend optionals.

Data center work is consuming the entire board: cloud infrastructure buildouts in the Midwest and Southeast are pulling hands faster than locals can feed them.

LINEMAN.text:
**IBEW Lineman Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** is the outlier—**$70.00/hr** top scale with 37 calls chasing just 63 hands. Fullford Electric posted Inside JW work at **$57.44** fresh today (July 7); Golden Valley Electric's Healy Powerhouse gig runs 8 weeks at **$30.86**, inventory work. Alaska Communications holds **$45.31** calls. Cold-weather premium and isolation math make this board worth the plane ticket if you can stomach 60+ days out.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** is packed—10 calls, 92 hands deep, but top scale sits at **$55.00** with all OT double time. Summit Line and Cupertino Electric both need NM State JIW License EE98J; Rosendin's offering **$40/hr** with **$15 incentive** for 90% of 60-hour weeks. This is grunt work, not top dollar, but steady.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 26 calls for 102 hands on Parsons' Polaris Forge Data Center—three separate crew calls, **$44.80/hr** with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** and weekend bonuses to **$150**. ND License required; all three calls are OPEN. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base addition at **$44.80** moves quick.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** shows 11 calls, 45 hands, **$65.33** scale. Prime Controls' Micron ID1 Inside JW hits **$45.33** plus **$20/hr** PLA/LARP wages and **$170/day per diem** if eligible over 75 miles—7-day eligibility. Quality Electric's St. Luke's Tower is a **$55.33** shop call, 5–8' work.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** runs three calls, 47 hands: ArchKey Sachs' Google Data Center West Memphis is the meat—**$36.65/hr**, 6am start, 4/10s and 2/8s, **$125/day incentive**. Book's moving.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** holds 25 calls, 32 hands, **$64.66** scale. Lineman work at Wanatah: 34kv and 12.5kv rebuilds, potential OT, no per diem.

Data-center and transmission work is absorbing the majority of board depth—Polaris, Google, Micron, Enovum—while legacy distribution rebuilds thin out. Hands are chasing incentives and per diem over raw scale.
